description: Cross-platform decrypt-at-rest CLI for files produced by protect. Auto-selects backend (DPAPI / Keychain / libsecret) from the WPRT header marker.

platform.value_on_windows: Header-based backend auto-dispatch: decrypts any protect-produced file without the caller remembering which scope was used.
platform.value_on_unix: Streaming decryption from stdin, implicit .prot-suffix stripping on output, and automatic Keychain/libsecret backend selection from the header marker.

prefer_default_when: portable encryption (cross-machine/user) — use age or gpg
encrypting structured data with external KMS — use sops
whole-disk or filesystem encryption — use FileVault, BitLocker, or LUKS

options.description: Structured JSON metadata for AI agents
Replace FILE atomically via temp + rename. Mutually exclusive with --output.
Delete source FILE after successful round-trip verification.
Retain source FILE (explicit default; accepted for symmetry with --rm).
Skip the post-encrypt round-trip integrity check (encrypt path only). Faster, less safe.

Overwrite an existing destination file. Without this flag, the tool refuses to clobber existing data. Symlink-safe: the destination is unlinked before exclusive create, so an attacker-planted symlink cannot redirect the write.
Explicit output path. Default: FILE with .prot stripped for file input, stdout for stdin.

Key-derivation scope. 'user' (default) — key bound to current OS user, decryptable only by that user on that machine. 'machine' — key bound to machine credential, decryptable by any user on that machine (Windows needs DPAPI LocalMachine access; macOS needs sudo for System Keychain; Linux: unsupported — tool exits with usage error).
Coloured output: auto (default when omitted), always, or never

exit_codes.description: Usage error: bad flags, path collision, --in-place without a FILE, unsupported scope on Linux
Runtime error: file not found, encryption/decryption failure, key store unavailable, round-trip verify failed, header magic mismatch, user/machine/scope mismatch on decrypt

io.stdin: Ciphertext (.prot stream) to decrypt when no FILE is given.
io.stdout: Plaintext when neither FILE nor -o is given (streaming mode).
io.stderr: Errors, retention warnings (plaintext not removed when --rm omitted), and diagnostic messages.

examples.description: Decrypt to an explicit output path
Decrypt then delete the ciphertext source
Decrypt an HMAC key in memory (never hits disk) and hash a payload

composes_with.description: Use an encrypted key without writing plaintext to disk
Decrypt and copy to the clipboard (pair with --rm for single-use tokens)
